description: Declarative correspondence between every module-level `verify_*` function in src/proofbundle/*.py (the ground truth, re-discovered live by AST in scripts/rust_parity_gate.py on every run) and the independent Rust second-verifier (tools/pb_verify_rs). This is the machine-checked replacement for a hand-maintained parity table (Finding 11): scripts/rust_parity_gate.py cross-checks every COVERED/PARTIAL claim here against main.rs's actual match arms, the built binary's self-declared coverage-report, and a literal call-site in crosscheck.py — a stale claim is caught, not trusted. A new verify_* function with no entry here shows up as UNTRACKED (a decision is owed), never silently 100%.

proofbundle.dsse.verify_envelope.notes: DSSE Ed25519 verify over the exact PAE bytes; crosscheck asserts a real Python-signed envelope verifies AND a tampered payload fails.

proofbundle.signature.verify_ed25519.notes: Ed25519 (RFC 8032) primitive used inside both verify-dsse and verify-bundle; exercised by both.

proofbundle.signature.verify_ecdsa_p256.notes: ES256/ECDSA P-256 (RFC 7518 3.4) issuer-signature primitive for SD-JWT VC (Finding 20, new in v3.2.3). No Rust implementation yet — parity decision: PENDING.

proofbundle.merkle.verify_inclusion.notes: RFC 6962/9162 inclusion-proof root recomputation; exercised via verify-bundle's internal root_from_inclusion on the native_bundle conformance corpus (real inclusion proofs). Not exposed as its own standalone subcommand.

proofbundle.bundle.verify_bundle.notes: Ed25519 signature + RFC 6962 merkle inclusion + relying-party root/tree-size + SD-JWT issuer-authenticity slice (no cnf / no eval-graft) are covered. NOT covered: Key Binding JWT (holder binding / replay-audience), cross-bundle sd-jwt binding (check_binds_bundle), external anchors (WP-A1 always reports unconfirmed, matching Python's own offline posture but not a full port), and the full sd_jwt_vc issuer-identity check.

proofbundle.trust_pack.verify_trust_pack.notes: root_threshold_met is ported for the Ed25519 leg only (Finding 11 first portation): counts DISTINCT non-revoked root key material with a valid Ed25519 signature over the DSSE PAE bytes, same aliasing-defense as trust_pack.py. NOT covered: mldsa65 / hybrid-ed25519-mldsa65 root keys (skipped, never silently accepted), not_expired, version_monotone / prevVersionDigest chaining, two-stage rotation authorization, and full predicate-shape validation.

proofbundle.merkle.verify_consistency.notes: RFC 9162 consistency-proof verification between two tree sizes; no Rust implementation.

proofbundle.decision.verify_decision_rec.notes: DSSE-signed Decision Receipt: crypto + structure over the exact signed bytes. Its content-root PRIMITIVE (jcs-sha256-v1) is independently cross-checked via the `content-root` subcommand against the decision/crossimpl corpus, but the full predicateType-confusion check, subject binding, and evidenceRefs validation are not ported.

proofbundle.outcome.verify_outcome_recei.notes: DSSE-signed Action Outcome Receipt: crypto + structure over the exact signed bytes. Shares the content-root primitive (covered) but its own predicateType/structure validation is not ported.

proofbundle.run_ledger.verify_run_ledger.notes: DSSE-signed Run Ledger: crypto + structure over the exact signed bytes. Shares the content-root primitive (covered) but its own predicateType/structure validation is not ported.

proofbundle.verification_summary.verify_.notes: DSSE-signed Verification Summary: crypto + structure over the exact signed bytes. Shares the content-root primitive (covered) but its own predicateType/structure validation is not ported.

proofbundle.intoto.verify_intoto_dsse.notes: DSSE-signed in-toto test-result attestation verify (export_intoto_dsse counterpart).

proofbundle.intoto.verify_eval_result_ds.notes: DSSE-signed eval-result attestation verify, incl. predicateType-confusion defense (WP-I1).

proofbundle.intoto.verify_svr_dsse.notes: DSSE-signed SVR (Sample Verification Record) attestation verify.

proofbundle.kbjwt.verify_key_binding.notes: Key Binding JWT (RFC 9901) verify of a compact SD-JWT presentation — holder proof-of-possession.

proofbundle.sdjwt.verify_sd_jwt.notes: Full SD-JWT compact-serialization verify (structure + optional issuer signature). A NARROW slice of this (issuer Ed25519 signature + eval-root-graft binding, no cnf) lives inside verify-bundle's verify_sdjwt_issuer and is reflected in bundle.verify_bundle's PARTIAL note, not here.

proofbundle.sdjwt_vc.verify_sdjwt_vc.notes: Full SD-JWT VC relying-party check: issuer signature AND the VC profile (vct/type binding, status list, disclosure structure).

proofbundle.statuslist.verify_status_sna.notes: Status List Token (bit-string revocation) snapshot verify, fully offline.

proofbundle.checkpoint.verify_checkpoint.notes: C2SP signed-checkpoint verify against a vkey (origin/tree_size/root).

proofbundle.checkpoint.verify_cosignatur.notes: One witness cosignature verify on a signed checkpoint note.

proofbundle.checkpoint.verify_witnessed_.notes: Checkpoint verify requiring BOTH log signature and a witness-quorum threshold.

proofbundle.anchors.verify_anchor.notes: Verify ONE external time-anchor entry, fail-closed, against target_roots.

proofbundle.anchors.verify_anchors.notes: Verify a receipt's whole `anchors` list (missing/empty -> SKIP unless required).

proofbundle.anchors_ots.verify_opentimes.notes: Fail-closed OpenTimestamps proof verify (confirmed/pending against a frozen Bitcoin block header). The RELATED security DECISION — a bundle's own frozen header is never self-trusted, so --require-anchor never confirms offline — IS reproduced by verify-bundle's anchor_rp_confirmed() (always false), but the OTS binary proof itself is not parsed.

proofbundle.anchors_rfc3161.verify_rfc31.notes: Fail-closed offline RFC 3161 (TSA) timestamp token verify.

proofbundle.anchors_markovian.verify_mar.notes: Fail-closed verifier for a markovian-provenance/v1 external anchor.

proofbundle.anchors_chia.verify_offline_.notes: Pure offline verification (level i) of a decoded chia-datalayer proof dict.

proofbundle.evidence_pack.verify_evidenc.notes: Evidence pack OFFLINE verify, delegates to the OTS verifier.

proofbundle.evalclaim.verify_commitment.notes: Salted-commitment check for one presented identifier in an eval claim (persample/pre-registration).

proofbundle.hashalg.verify_dual_hash.notes: Verify every declared digest binds the data AND at least one is a current algorithm (crypto-agility).

proofbundle.hf_evals.verify_receipt_toke.notes: Unpack and verify a `pb1.` HuggingFace-eval receipt token.

proofbundle.hf_evals.verify_eval_results.notes: Verifier-side check of one .eval_results entry (WP-I2 value/verdict binding).

proofbundle.persample.verify_sample_open.notes: Verify one per-sample opening against the receipt's committed samples root, offline, fail-closed.

proofbundle.pqsig.verify_mldsa.notes: ML-DSA (FIPS 204) signature verify — needs a PQ crate; none is vendored in pb_verify_rs.

proofbundle.pqsig.verify_slhdsa.notes: SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) signature verify — Python-side marked OPTIONAL/currently OPEN itself.

proofbundle.pqsig.verify_hybrid.notes: Hybrid Ed25519+ML-DSA verify (BOTH legs must verify) — depends on verify_mldsa (PENDING).

proofbundle.prereg.verify_prereg.notes: Check that a claim's prereg_sha256 matches the sha256 of the pre-registration protocol file.

proofbundle.renewal.verify_sequence.notes: ArchiveTimeStampSequence end-to-end verify, walking the newest strong anchor back to the origin.

proofbundle.tlogproof.verify_tlog_proof.notes: tlog-proof (transparency-log inclusion) offline verify against explicit trust anchors.

proofbundle.evalcard.verify_evaluation_c.notes: Evaluation Card digest resolve+match (Finding 18, new in v3.2.3). No Rust implementation yet — parity decision: PENDING.

proofbundle.relation.verify_relationship.notes: relation/v0.1 lineage-edge chain verification (EXPERIMENTAL, 3.3.0): pure offline cycle/depth walk over attached targets. The Rust core does not carry the profile yet; the design note declares the differential test NOT_RUN until it does.
